name: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark
description: Microsoft's consolidated security best-practice benchmark for Azure workloads. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Establish network segmentation boundaries
controls.summary: Workloads should be segmented with VNets, subnets, and NSGs so a compromise in one tier cannot freely reach another.

check_hints.fail_message: No virtual network segmentation is present in the architecture.

controls.remediation: Place workloads in VNets with tiered subnets, apply NSGs with least-privilege rules, and route inter-tier traffic through inspection where required.

controls.title: Secure PaaS services with private networking
controls.summary: PaaS services holding data (SQL, Storage, Key Vault) should use Private Endpoints and disable public network access.
controls.remediation: Create Private Endpoints for data services, disable public network access, and use Private DNS zones.

controls.title: Deploy DDoS protection
controls.summary: Internet-facing workloads should be protected against volumetric attacks.
controls.remediation: Use Azure DDoS Network Protection on VNets with public IPs, or rely on Front Door's built-in edge protection for HTTP workloads.

controls.title: Deploy a web application firewall
controls.summary: Public web applications must sit behind a WAF inspecting for common exploitation patterns.

check_hints.fail_message: No Web Application Firewall protects the public entry point.

controls.remediation: Front public apps with Application Gateway WAF v2 or Front Door WAF in Prevention mode using managed rule sets.

controls.title: Centralize identity with Entra ID
controls.summary: All human and workload identity should be governed by a single Entra ID tenant rather than local accounts.

check_hints.fail_message: No centralized identity provider is present in the architecture.

controls.remediation: Integrate applications with Entra ID (OIDC/SAML), eliminate local account stores, and manage access via groups and roles.

controls.title: Use managed identities for workload authentication
controls.summary: Applications should authenticate to Azure resources with managed identities, eliminating stored credentials.

check_hints.fail_message: Workload uses stored credentials instead of a Managed Identity.

controls.remediation: Assign managed identities and grant least-privilege RBAC; remove secrets and connection strings from configuration.

controls.title: Use strong, phishing-resistant authentication
controls.summary: Require MFA everywhere; prefer FIDO2 or certificate-based methods for privileged access.
controls.remediation: Enforce MFA via Conditional Access; roll out passkeys/FIDO2 for admins first, then broadly.

controls.title: Limit and monitor highly privileged roles
controls.summary: Global/privileged role assignments should be minimal, eligible (not permanent), and reviewed.

check_hints.fail_message: Privileged roles are permanently assigned without PIM just-in-time elevation.

controls.remediation: Use Entra Privileged Identity Management for eligible assignments, approval workflows, and access reviews.

controls.title: Follow just-enough-administration (least privilege)
controls.summary: RBAC assignments should be scoped to the minimum role and scope required.
controls.remediation: Replace Owner/Contributor grants with task-scoped built-in roles at the narrowest scope; audit with Azure Policy.

controls.title: Encrypt sensitive data in transit
controls.summary: All service-to-service and client traffic must use TLS 1.2+.
controls.remediation: Enforce HTTPS/TLS 1.2+ on every hop; disable plaintext protocol endpoints.

controls.title: Enable data-at-rest encryption
controls.summary: Platform encryption should be verified on all data stores; use customer-managed keys where compliance requires.
controls.remediation: Verify service-side encryption is active; configure CMK in Key Vault with rotation for regulated data.

controls.title: Secure the key and secret management lifecycle
controls.summary: Keys, secrets, and certificates belong in Key Vault with restricted network access, RBAC, rotation, and purge protection.

check_hints.fail_message: No managed secret store (Key Vault) is present in the architecture.

controls.remediation: Centralize secrets in Key Vault, enable purge protection and firewall, rotate credentials automatically, and access via managed identity.

controls.title: Enable threat detection capabilities
controls.summary: Defender for Cloud plans and analytics should cover all workload resource types.

check_hints.fail_message: No threat detection service is present.

controls.remediation: Enable relevant Defender plans; integrate alerts with Microsoft Sentinel or your SIEM.

controls.title: Centralize logging and retention
controls.summary: Resource, activity, and application logs should flow to a central Log Analytics workspace with defined retention.

check_hints.fail_message: No centralized log workspace is present in the architecture.

controls.remediation: Create a central Log Analytics workspace, wire diagnostic settings from all resources, and set retention per compliance needs.

controls.title: Prepare incident notification and response
controls.summary: Security contacts, alert routing, and an incident response runbook must exist before an incident.
controls.remediation: Set Defender for Cloud security contacts, route alerts to on-call, and maintain IR runbooks mapped to likely Azure scenarios.

controls.title: Audit and enforce secure configurations
controls.summary: Azure Policy should continuously audit and enforce baseline configuration across subscriptions.
controls.remediation: Assign the MCSB policy initiative in Azure Policy, remediate non-compliance, and gate deployments with policy-as-code in CI.
